lesson.title: Lost Toy: Mr Dinosaur
lesson.summary: In this episode, George loses his favorite toy, Mr Dinosaur, and Peppa becomes a detective to find it.

grammar_concepts.name: Present Simple
grammar_concepts.structure_rules: Subject + verb (base form for I/you/we/they, add -s/-es for he/she/it). Negative: subject + do/does + not + base verb. Questions: Do/Does + subject + base verb?
grammar_concepts.context_usage: Used to express facts, habits, routines, and general truths. In the transcript, it describes George's likes and routines.
grammar_concepts.learning_tips: Pay attention to the third person singular -s/-es ending. Pronunciation varies: /s/ after voiceless sounds (likes), /z/ after voiced sounds (loves), /ɪz/ after sibilants (kisses).
grammar_concepts.explanation: Subject + verb (base form for I/you/we/they, add -s/-es for he/she/it). Negative: subject + do/does + not + base verb. Questions: Do/Does + subject + base verb?
examples_from_transcript: Sometimes George likes to scare Peppa with Mr Dinosaur.

grammar_concepts.name: Wh-Questions
grammar_concepts.structure_rules: Wh-word (what, where, when, why, who, how) + auxiliary verb (be, do, have) + subject + main verb (or complement). For questions with 'to be': Wh-word + be + subject + ...?
grammar_concepts.context_usage: Used to ask for specific information. In the transcript, Peppa asks about the detective and Mr Dinosaur's location.
grammar_concepts.learning_tips: Intonation usually rises at the end of the question. Remember that the auxiliary verb changes for different tenses (e.g., 'is' for present, 'was' for past).
grammar_concepts.explanation: Wh-word (what, where, when, why, who, how) + auxiliary verb (be, do, have) + subject + main verb (or complement). For questions with 'to be': Wh-word + be + subject + ...?

grammar_concepts.name: Possessive 's
grammar_concepts.structure_rules: Possessor noun + 's + possessed noun (e.g., George's toy). For plural possessors ending in -s, add only an apostrophe (e.g., parents' house).
grammar_concepts.context_usage: Indicates ownership or a close relationship between a person/animal and something. In the transcript, it shows what belongs to George.
grammar_concepts.learning_tips: The pronunciation of 's follows the same rules as plural -s: /s/ after voiceless, /z/ after voiced, /ɪz/ after sibilants. Be careful not to confuse with contractions (e.g., George's vs. George is).
grammar_concepts.explanation: Possessor noun + 's + possessed noun (e.g., George's toy). For plural possessors ending in -s, add only an apostrophe (e.g., parents' house).
examples_from_transcript: George's favourite toy is Mr Dinosaur.
George's favourite game is throwing Mr Dinosaur up in the air.

vocabulary.definition: a prehistoric reptile that lived millions of years ago; in the transcript, it refers to George's toy named Mr Dinosaur.
vocabulary.example_sentence: George's favourite toy is Mr Dinosaur.

vocabulary.definition: a person whose job is to find information or solve mysteries, especially by asking questions and looking for clues.
vocabulary.example_sentence: A detective is a very important person who is good at finding things.
Sometimes George likes to scare Peppa with Mr Dinosaur.

vocabulary.definition: placed snugly or securely, often referring to bedding or covering.
vocabulary.example_sentence: When George goes to bed, Mr Dinosaur is tucked up with him.

exercises.question: What is George's favourite toy?
exercises.explanation: In the transcript, George's favourite toy is Mr Dinosaur.

exercises.question: Peppa says she is a good ______.
exercises.explanation: Peppa claims she is good at finding things, and Daddy Pig confirms she is the detective.

exercises.question: Mr Dinosaur was lost in the garden.
exercises.explanation: Mr Dinosaur was found up in a tree in the garden after being lost.

exercises.question: What does George sometimes do with Mr Dinosaur to scare Peppa?
exercises.explanation: George roars 'Grrrrr!' to scare Peppa with Mr Dinosaur.

exercises.question: George ______ (love) Mr Dinosaur.
exercises.explanation: Present simple third person singular adds -s: loves.

exercises.question: Daddy Pig asks, 'Where ______ Mr Dinosaur?'
exercises.explanation: Wh-question with 'where' and singular subject 'Mr Dinosaur' uses 'is'.

exercises.question: George's favourite toy is Mr Dinosaur.
exercises.explanation: Possessive 's shows ownership: George's favourite toy.

exercises.question: Who helps George look for Mr Dinosaur?
exercises.explanation: Peppa is the detective and Daddy Pig helps; Mummy Pig is not shown helping.

exercises.question: At first, Peppa guesses Mr Dinosaur is in the ______.
exercises.explanation: Peppa first guesses the bath because George always has Mr Dinosaur in the bath.

exercises.question: George throws Mr Dinosaur up in the air and catches him.
exercises.explanation: George's favourite game is throwing Mr Dinosaur up and catching him.

source_text.full_text: I'm Peppa Pig. This is my little brother George. This is Mummy Pig. And this is Daddy Pig. Peppa Pig. Mr Dinosaur is lost. George's favourite toy is Mr Dinosaur. Dinosaur. George loves Mr Dinosaur. Grrrrr! Sometimes George likes to scare Peppa with Mr Dinosaur. Grrrrr! Eee! Too scary! At supper time, Mr Dinosaur sits next to George. I beg your pardon. Was that you, George? Or was it Mr Dinosaur? Dinosaur. At bath time, George shares his bath with Mr Dinosaur. Good night, Peppa. Good night, Mummy. Good night, George. And good night, Mr Dinosaur. When George goes to bed, Mr Dinosaur is tucked up with him. George's favourite game is throwing Mr Dinosaur up in the air. Whee! And catching him when he falls back down. Whee! Whee! Peppa and Daddy Pig are playing draughts. I win, Daddy! Oh! Well done, Peppa. George! George, what's the matter? I'm sore. George, have you lost Mr Dinosaur? George has lost Mr Dinosaur. Don't worry, George. We'll find Mr Dinosaur. It's a job for a detective. Daddy, what is a dee-tech-tive? A detective is a very important person who is good at finding things. Me, me! I'm good at finding things. Alright. Peppa is the detective. George, I'm the detective. I will help you find Mr Dinosaur. Maybe the detective should ask George some simple questions. George, where's Mr Dinosaur? George does not know where Mr Dinosaur is. The detective could try and guess where Mr Dinosaur might be. I know, I know where he is. George always has Mr Dinosaur with him in the bath. So Mr Dinosaur is in the bath. Mr Dinosaur is not in the bath. Oh. I know! I know where Mr Dinosaur is. George always has Mr Dinosaur in his bed at night. See, that's where he is. Mr Dinosaur is not in George's bed. Oh. Maybe we should try the garden. Yes, the garden! I was going to say that. Where is Mr Dinosaur? Mr Dinosaur is very hard to find. Oh, Mr Dinosaur isn't anywhere. George, you do love to throw Mr Dinosaur in the air. I wonder if this time you threw Mr Dinosaur just a bit too high. There he is! There he is! I saw him first! Well done, Peppa. You really are a very good detective. I'm so. grown! George is so happy to have Mr Dinosaur back again. Maybe it isn't a good idea to play with dinosaurs near trees. Dinosaurs.
